successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Curated from official Fordham University alumni, academic-department, graduate-school, and law-school profile pages, prioritizing person-specific stories with explicit degree-year evidence and substantive post-graduation impact.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official Fordham University pages on fordham.edu, including School of Law alumni-of-distinction pages, Honors alumni profiles, GSAS alumni spotlights, and a Theology notable-alumni profile.

items.name: Geraldine Ferraro
items.bio: Fordham School of Law says Geraldine Ferraro received her law degree in 1960, became the first woman vice presidential candidate on a major party ticket in 1984, and previously built a public career as a prosecutor and member of Congress.
items.description: Congresswoman, prosecutor, and 1984 Democratic vice-presidential nominee.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Fordham School of Law alumni-of-distinction page with explicit law-degree year and substantial career summary.

items.name: Eunice Carter
items.bio: Fordham School of Law says Eunice Carter graduated from Fordham Law’s evening program in 1932 and in 1935 became the first Black woman assistant district attorney in New York state, later helping secure Charles 'Lucky' Luciano’s conviction.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Fordham School of Law alumni-of-distinction page with explicit graduation-year evidence and substantive impact summary.

items.name: Ruth Whitehead Whaley
items.bio: Fordham School of Law says Ruth Whitehead Whaley graduated at the top of her class in 1924 and became the first Black woman to practice law in New York state after first becoming the first Black woman to enroll at and graduate from Fordham Law.
items.description: Trailblazing attorney and founder of the Black Business and Professional Women’s Club.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Fordham School of Law alumni-of-distinction page with explicit graduation-year evidence and substantive career summary.

items.name: Felipe N. Torres
items.bio: Fordham School of Law says Felipe N. Torres graduated from Fordham in 1926, became the first Puerto Rican assemblyman elected from the Bronx, and later was named the first Puerto Rican judge on the New York State Family Court.
items.description: Lawyer, legislator, and pioneering Puerto Rican jurist in New York.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Fordham School of Law alumni-of-distinction page with explicit graduation-year evidence and substantial public-service summary.

items.name: John Sexton
items.bio: Fordham’s Theology department says John Sexton earned a Fordham B.A. in History in 1963, an M.A. in Comparative Religion in 1965, and a Ph.D. in History of American Religion in 1978 before becoming president of New York University and dean emeritus of NYU Law School.
items.description: Higher-education leader and former president of New York University.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Fordham department alumni page with explicit Fordham degree years and extensive career summary.

items.name: Antonio Del Pino
items.bio: Fordham Honors says Antonio Del Pino, FCRH ’91, earned his B.A. from Fordham in 1991 and became a partner and global chair of Latham & Watkins’ Latin America Practice after building a cross-border legal career.
items.description: Global corporate lawyer and Latin America practice leader at Latham & Watkins.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Fordham Honors alumni page with explicit degree-year evidence and current leadership role.

items.name: Natalie Kinnear
items.bio: Fordham Honors says Natalie Kinnear, FCRH ’22, graduated from Fordham in 2022 with an international political economy major and has worked across design research, English teaching in South Korea, and client services at S&P Global.
items.description: Recent alumna working in market intelligence after international teaching and design-research roles.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Fordham Honors alumni page with explicit class-year evidence and clear early-career trajectory.

items.name: Nicole Rafanello
items.bio: Fordham GSAS says Nicole Rafanello, GSAS ’05, earned a Ph.D. in Psychology in 2005 and built a career as a licensed clinical and forensic psychologist, including helping people navigate difficult life transitions.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Fordham GSAS alumni spotlight with explicit Ph.D. year and substantive career summary.

items.name: Tom Hughes
items.bio: Fordham GSAS says Tom Hughes, GSAS ’79, earned an M.A. in Theology in 1979 and went on to serve as executive vice president, general counsel, and corporate secretary at Greater New York Mutual Insurance Company while also teaching philosophy.
items.description: Insurance-company general counsel and adjunct philosophy professor.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Fordham GSAS alumni spotlight with explicit degree-year evidence and detailed legal-career summary.

items.name: Jared Woodard
items.bio: Fordham GSAS says Jared Woodard, GSAS ’13, earned a Ph.D. in Philosophy in 2013 and became head of the research investment committee at Bank of America after moving from academic philosophy into macroeconomic and markets analysis.
items.description: Bank of America research-investment leader and former global investment strategist.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Fordham GSAS alumni spotlight with explicit Ph.D. year and substantive finance-career summary.

override_warnings: Fordham alumni depth was manually improved because the generic pipeline only surfaced a shallow alumni list.
This override prioritizes official Fordham pages with explicit degree-year or class-year evidence where available.
